formula_policy: Use menu_labor_cost_proxy as temporary direct labor cost base for economics v1 before assumption-driven hourly wage engine is activated.
reason: Keeps v1 stable and aligned with locked pricing sheet without prematurely forcing hourly labor assumptions.
next_upgrade_path: Replace with duration_hours * hourly_loaded_therapist_cost when payroll engine is finalized.

formula_policy: Do not separately add employer SSC in v1 if labor proxy already reflects labor burden logic indirectly; keep SSC as documented official input for later formula-driven version.
reason: Avoids double counting before labor formula decomposition is explicitly built.
next_upgrade_path: Break out SSC explicitly once hourly labor base and worker category logic are implemented.

formula_policy: Use menu_overhead_cost_proxy as temporary overhead allocation base for economics v1.
reason: Maintains consistency with locked menu economics while rent benchmark is still draft-ready, not final.
next_upgrade_path: Replace with rent- and capacity-driven hourly overhead absorption after occupancy logic is finalized.

formula_policy: Use menu_material_cost_proxy as temporary material cost input for economics v1.
reason: Material research is not yet finalized, so proxy-first keeps structure moving without fake precision.
next_upgrade_path: Replace with treatment-level consumable cost once material dataset is captured.

formula_policy: Keep VAT reference documented but do not force a new conversion layer in v1 if menu prices are already handled on a commercial basis.
reason: Avoids unnecessary distortion before explicit ex-VAT / inc-VAT pricing logic is introduced.
next_upgrade_path: Add explicit VAT conversion once pricing basis is formally locked as inclusive or exclusive.

formula_policy: Populate base case first. Keep low/high scenario columns empty until formula-driven inputs are more mature.
reason: Prevents fake scenario complexity before cost drivers are decomposed properly.
next_upgrade_path: Activate low/high scenarios after hourly labor, utilization, and overhead logic are formalized.
